chapter_context.summary: Manasseh reigns wickedly for 55 years, introducing detestable idolatry and shedding innocent blood; Amon succeeds him wickedly and is assassinated by his servants.

outline.text: Manasseh's Horrific Fifty-Five Year Evil Reign; Rebuilds Altars to Baal
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Manasseh's Horrific Fifty-Five Year Evil Reign; Rebuilds Altars to Baal (21:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Prophets Foretell Absolute Destruction of Jerusalem; Death of Manasseh
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Prophets Foretell Absolute Destruction of Jerusalem; Death of Manasseh (21:10-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Amon's Evil Reign; Assassinated; Josiah Made King
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Amon's Evil Reign; Assassinated; Josiah Made King (21:19-26), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

key_themes.title: Prophetic Authority
key_themes.description: Elisha carries the mantle of Elijah, showing that God's word stands firm in judgment and mercy regardless of political power.

key_themes.title: Covenant Faithfulness and Exile
key_themes.description: The tragic fall of Samaria and Jerusalem shows that persistent rebellion and idolatry inevitably lead to judgment.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 21 prompt you to trust in God's promises even during seasons of spiritual decline?
What warnings or instructions does this chapter hold for guarding your own heart against syncretism?

cultural_historical.title: The Assyrian and Babylonian Empires
cultural_historical.text: The rise of Mesopotamian super-empires (Assyria and Babylon) serves as the historical backdrop for the exile and division of the Hebrew kingdoms.
